stories.title: Quoting Sean Lynch
stories.paragraph: Simon Willison highlighted a Hacker News comment from Sean Lynch arguing that the most practical value of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) is its ability to isolate authentication flows outside an agent's context window, and potentially outside the execution harness entirely. Lynch suggests that even a stripped-down version of MCP functioning solely as an auth gateway for APIs would represent a net improvement over current approaches. The observation reframes MCP's significance away from broad tool-integration narratives and toward a narrower, more concrete problem: keeping credentials and auth state out of the token stream that language models process.
